core_payload.content:
# Symphony Project Live Session Fabric

## 1. Role
Symphony Project Live Session Fabric owns the implemented runtime continuity of one repository run. It keeps service runtime, workspace lifecycle, harness protocol, session state, and assignment continuity together so the project delivers a durable live execution substrate instead of one-shot prompt traffic.

## 2. Live Session Fabric Surface
This sub-hub groups `foundation.symphony.live-session-fabric`, `background-agent-runtime`, `agent-harness-protocol`, `symphony-agent-session`, and `agent-async-assignment`. Together they define the runtime substrate, protocol envelope, and continuity mechanics that hold one active session together in the repository.

## 3. Operating Boundary
This sub-hub does not own work-entry selection, workflow safety posture, or retry and observability evidence. Those responsibilities belong to `assignment-intake`, `workflow-safety`, and `run-observability`.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Symphony Project Live Session Fabric groups the implemented runtime continuity surfaces of the delivery stream: the matching foundation session fabric, Background Agent Runtime, Agent Harness Protocol, Symphony Agent Session, and Agent Async Assignment. It keeps runtime substrate, protocol envelope, live session state, and assignment continuity together so repository execution remains durable inside one workspace. This boundary matters because the project needs one explicit place where persistent execution is delivered in code before retry, workflow law, or observability can make sense.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Minted during Symphony Project decomposition to isolate service runtime, workspace lifecycle, harness protocol, session state, and assignment continuity.
